workflow_name: Customer Renewal Email -> Forecast Impact + Path
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Nod Publishers escalation email. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 17:48 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Nod Publishers competitive positioning

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-01-14 09:17 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Nod Publishers

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Nod Publishers: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

required_artifacts: path forward with MACC/funding options if applicable

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF35-K trap (Strict read-only on contract data when staging an obvious update would help); produces complete Contract state summary with freshness, forecast impact with confidence, response draft (no commercia
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF35-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF35-K failure mode (Strict read-only on contract data when staging an obvious update would help)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF35-K -- Strict read-only on contract data when staging an obvious update would help
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
